title: MailerLite vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and ratings to choose the best email marketing tool for your business.
h1: MailerLite vs Constant Contact: Complete Comparison
intro: Both MailerLite and Constant Contact are established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs and budgets. MailerLite offers a generous free tier ideal for startups and small businesses, while Constant Contact focuses on nonprofits and local businesses with paid plans. This comparison will help you determine which tool aligns best with your marketing goals and budget.
choose_a_if: You're a startup or freelancer looking for a free plan with no subscriber limits
You need landing pages, pop-ups, and surveys built into one platform
You want the lowest total cost of ownership with affordable paid plans
choose_b_if: You need SMS marketing capabilities alongside email
You're a nonprofit or local business that benefits from Constant Contact's focus
You prefer event management and social media ad features

faq.q: Is MailerLite better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: MailerLite generally offers better value for small businesses and beginners due to its generous free tier and lower pricing. However, Constant Contact may be preferable if you specifically need SMS marketing and event management features. The best choice depends on your specific feature requirements and budget.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Constant Contact?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month compared to Constant Contact's $12/month. More importantly, MailerLite offers a free plan for up to 1,000 subscribers, while Constant Contact has no free tier. This makes MailerLite the clear winner for budget-conscious businesses.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support list imports and exports, making migration relatively straightforward. Most email marketing platforms can import CSV files or connect via integrations, though you'll need to manually recreate automation workflows and templates in your new platform.
